name: Zesty Lemon Granita
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring 2 cups water to a boil in a medium, nonreactive saucepan.
2. Add the lemon juice, lemon zest, and sugar to the boiling water.
3. Simmer for about 30 minutes to blend the flavors.
4. Traditional Method: Transfer the syrup to a medium, nonreactive bowl and cool to room temperature. Freeze the mixture, stirring every 30 minutes until it is just solid (3 to 4 hours).
5. No-Stir Method: Transfer the syrup to a nonreactive container large enough for the mixture to come 3/4 inch up the side. Cool to room temperature and then freeze solid. Remove from the freezer and let stand at room temperature for 5 minutes. Invert the pan onto a cutting surface, remove it, and cut the frozen mixture into small pieces with a metal pastry scraper or large knife. Chop the pieces into tiny granules.
6. Garnish: Cut lemon zest into fine julienne strips. Cover with water and bring to boil for 5 minutes; drain. Combine 1 cup sugar, 1/3 cup corn syrup, and 1/3 cup water in a saucepan and bring to boil. Remove from heat, stir in zest, and let stand for 30 minutes. Transfer the zest to a wire rack. When it feels dry after 15 minutes, roll it in additional sugar.
7. Serving: Scoop the granita into chilled serving glasses and garnish with candied lemon zest. Serve immediately. Makes 6 servings.
